title: oenothera missouriensis var. oklahomensis
intro: Oenothera missouriensis var. oklahomensis, commonly known as Oklahoma evening primrose, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Oklahoma evening primrose include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Oklahoma evening primrose is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in herbal medicine to soothe and calm the skin, as well as to support overall health and well-being.
